6-science-ch1.label: Ch 1: The Wonderful World of Science
6-science-ch1.title: Class 6 Science: Ch 1 - The Wonderful World of Science

questions.q: According to the opening chapter of the textbook, what is the primary and fundamental definition of 'Science'?
options: A method of memorizing long definitions for school examinations.
A way of thinking, observing, and doing things to understand our world and uncover the secrets of the universe.
The process of building large mechanical calculating machines.
Strictly copying diagrams from a whiteboard into a textbook.
questions.explain: Science is a structured way of thinking, observing, and doing things to discover how the world works.

questions.q: What is considered the most important human trait or quality required to start the fascinating journey of science, from which this textbook gets its title?
options: Curiosity
Speed typing
Silent reading
Perfect handwriting
questions.explain: Curiosity is the core engine of scientific discovery, as emphasized by the textbook title.

questions.q: When we observe natural phenomena keenly, what are we naturally prompted to do as budding scientists?
options: Ignore them and focus only on indoor activities.
Pose questions and ask exactly 'how' and 'why' things happen.
Assume that these secrets can never be understood by humans.
Calculate the exact market value of those natural resources.
questions.explain: Keen observation naturally sparks questions, encouraging exploration of nature's mysteries.

questions.q: The textbook mentions that science is rarely done by a single individual alone. How do scientists across the world typically prefer to work?
options: They work completely isolated from each other in secret locations.
They work together in cooperative, large collaborative teams.
They compete to hide their information from other countries.
They leave all the work entirely to automated computer systems.
questions.explain: Science is collaborative; scientists share data and work together as teams.

questions.q: The chapter concludes with a clever wordplay: 'to be a wise person, you must be a...' complete the sentence as per the textbook's message.
options: 'how' person
'whys' person
'silent' person
'rich' person
questions.explain: The textbook says: 'to be a wise person, you must be a whys person!'
